Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 281 • Updated: 2026-04-09 18:06 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62079750
>>62079649
Literally THE BEST chain currently. And no, I'm not just making shit up. It literally does what everyone else is trying to do. Once JAM + NOMT hit it'll be able to do MILLIONS of TPS (not just value tx) while doing continous compute, while being 100% decentalized, while the tx will be CHEAP, while having NATIVE interoperability, while being able to control other chains and never leave DOT, ... This projects is THE project and people are still buying meme coins. It's UNFATHOMABLE.

62080914
I hope you guys smell the bottom. I can smell the bottom of the Vishnu mindchain. I hope you're going in HARD and DEEP. I hope you guys are PENETRATING THE BOTTOM of the market. I hope you're bouncing up and down on that bottom. I hope you're eating that bottom out and scooping it up. Mmmm, that delicious Vishnu bottom. It has a great taste inside my mouth. I hope you guys are enjoying it as much as I am. I'll leave some cream for you so it tastes even better.

62080937
>>62079649
DOT is literally the most superior blockchain out there. Everything that other people promise and don't deliver DOT already has. Everything that other people lie about and can't do DOT can do. The really BIG upgrade we're waiting on is JAM and NOMT this year
Read more here >>62026407 and >>62056612 and >>62056570. NOMT + JAM scale the network to more a few million or maybe 10-20m TPS. We currently have the highest recorded TPS on a live network (https://data.parity.io/spammening). 143.3k TPS on a live network (NOT TEST NET BULLSHITERY like the other chains are showcasing). This was done one 23 of the 100 cores that are available. So this was only 23% of the full capacity. Some cores are already sold to other chains therefore we can't do a full test. But you can guesstimate the numbers, right? And since the last spammening the throughput has gone up. We could now probably do like 250k TPS from 23 cores or something like that. Just look at the "Performance Charts" on the page I linked. The fees were literally almost always below 0.05$. The fees were around 0.03 to 0.015$ even when the network handled 140k TPS. Do you remember how Solana halted the network literally 8x due to it being congested and shit like that? That can't happen on DOT. The average finality was 16.58s. The thing is though, we'll soon be getting 500ms blocks. When the tests were conducted the block times were still 12s? Now they're 6s and soon they'll be 500ms (Basti blocks). A lot is happening on DOT this year!
Also if you look at the stats the network has been growing over time https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily

62080949
>>62079649
Also combine that with the new tokenomics and the long term vision for the project... I really don't see this project becoming irrelevant to be honest. It simply JUST FUCKING DOES WHAT IT'S SUPPOSED TO DO. The other L2s are behind and they won't catch up ever in my opinion. Once JAM hits DOT will be AT LEAST 5 to 10 years ahead of all the major L2s and L1s. It's not even comparable to be honest. People are blind to not see it. Sooner or later people will wake up and realize "OMG, I've been a degenerate memecoin gambler while I could buy the Vishnu of the blockchain".
Simply put, this project does what others promise and don't deliver. DOT has already DELIVERED and is DELIVERING even more amazing things that no other chain has and if they do they don't package it like DOT does. Sure ICP has continous compute, but does it have native interoperability and can host L2s and smart contracts and has 500ms block times and is decentralized and has shared security to his L2s and has the best governance system in crypto and has over a few million TPS and has ... and has ... Bro, it's fucking impossible to beat DOT on technical superiority. Some day people will realize that they can't build a new use case on the other chains. That they're LIMITING THEMSELVES by staying on shit like ARB or POL or some other fucking shit like that. A few months back Polkadot Asset Hub launched and people can simply copy paste EVM code to DOT and it'll work. We're currently waiting for this referendum (https://polkadot.polkassembly.io/referenda/1864) to pass in order for Uniswap and Safe to launch on Asset Hub. Other major projects will probably follow. Should pass any day now. Slow and steady wins the race

62081930

>>62080914
>>62080937
>>62080949

Why should we believe some one from India

62082053
>>62081930
Truth is not to be believed. Truth is to be seen. Just try the chain out. And look at the data. I've provided you with plenty of evidence. You SHOULD NOT believe me. The essence of crypto is what? DYOR. Do Your Own Research. We really did forget our roots, didn't we?

Vishnu Kumar

Polkadot uses Rust—specifically, modern, full-featured Rust—rather than a restricted subset of the language.

>and then scrambling to make an EVM to attract more developers
Actually, even this is not completely really true to be honest. The whole point of DOT 1.0 was to be able to make L2s. That was done and complete and there's like 40 L2s running on DOT. Then DOT 2.0 came and with it came Asset hub. Asset hub is a COMMON GOOD L2 that can run, as you've mentioned, EVM smart contracts and also PVM smart contracts which are SUPERIOR to EVM because the use the RISC-V architecture. You literally make it seem like launching EVM compatibility is a bad choice where in reality it's a move forward in order to make the chain more flexible.

https://polkadot.polkassembly.io/referenda/1864 Deploy Universal Deterministic Deployment Proxy (CREATE2 Factory) on Asset Hub
This ref passed today making it so that people can launch their EVM smart contracts on the same address as they have on ETH or other L2s. So Uniswap will soon launch on Asset Hub on the same address as they're on ETH or any other L2.

item.title: any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management… — 44 replies — 76.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management…

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-04-09 17:38 UTC

OP

any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job
I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management experience but im not getting any offers on my applications
Should I get some cyber security certs? which ones should I focus on if yes?

Early replies (first 3)

62084718
Funny of you to ask OP, I remember my struggles of landing my first WFH job. Btw, WFH stands for Work from Home because not many people come across that term usually. But, if you must ask, our company is headquartered in Nashville. My base pay is 95k per year along with a COL stipend and generous health and pension benefits. As we speak, I am clocked into my position in the beautiful hot springs in British Columbia. We do have an RTO policy, but I haven't been to the office in 4 years! Ha-Ha! You COULDN'T pay me to work in the office. Yesterday I had fun fucking a uni student that transferred from china for the school years, so yeah life is great so far. Today she is making me her traditional Chinese dinner as a thank you for fucking her so nicely. Maybe next month I'll fly out to Holland and do my job for the month. But like I said OP, keep your chin up and don't be disuaded by the multiple rejection emails in your future until you get that golden offer. Take it and run with it. Anyways .... Chiao!

62084729
>>62084718
thanks for the response anon
Did you use indeed or pay for a website that specializes in WFH jobs like flexjobs etc? I'm wondering if indeed is just all scams at this point and if it might be worth it to pay a job finding website to filter the garbage

62084746
There are no jobs anymore. 200,000 people have been laid off in the last six months while job creation is negative. 90% of job openings go to immigrants anyway.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62085904
>>62085875
Exactly this. OP should focus their attention on destroying glowfucks and not something that only neckbeard redditors want to do. Cybersecurity was always fake and for fags

62086811
>>62084759
>They hire new people every day faggot
yeah in hyderabad

62086826
>>62084746
Why you call them “immigrants” man? Those are not immigrants, those are pure turdies in turdworld
It’s called off sourcing

62087064
>>62084678
green id get

62087137
>>62084678
got it years ago

62087226
>>62085899
Thank you anon. Sounds like a plan, I am currently learning Python, is SQL hard to learn?
So basically I should look for jobs as Data Analyst, can I try to apply even without any experience in the field? I have been working as a development engineer since 7 years and looking to change careers into 100% WFH.

62087308
>>62087226
SQL is definitely easier than python. If you’re not a complete retard it shouldn’t take too long to become proficient. And yeah you can apply without any experience, data analyst is usually an entry level role. Just find some datasets online and make some dashboards that look nice and say you know SQL, that was enough for me to get a job. It’s not a bad career path because you can transition into data engineering pretty easily from data analyst and data engineers can make a lot of money at the right companies.

62087410
>>62084678
Why are you people still keeping the belief that wfh jobs are attainable alive? Its a complete scam, especially now.

>>62085904
>>62085875
This is right
>t. Fell for the cyber security meme because I thought I would be able to wfh

3 years later and I never got a job in the field let alone a wfh one. I fell for the scam. Don't do what I did.

62088425
>>62085875
I lucked into one of those roles and it sucked (apart from having ok pay and being WFH)
I'm sure people can succeed in that role but it's one of those roles where there's an autistic degree of variance in terms of responsibility and buy-in from the rest of the org, to most orgs it's just a cost center that gets in the way of productivity

62088526
>>62084678
I got laid off from my last job in March 2025. I was WFH, thanks to covid we all went WFH. 9 years at the company and genuinely the top guy. I was team lead, I did the training, I wrote the manuals, I had the highest numbers. I worked in fraud prevention for ecomm. I went to meetings with the big dogs while my manager who knew less about our programs stayed with the rest of the team. I loved my bosses. They loved me. I got laid off after training 10 Filipinos. So did the rest of our NA Fraud team and our manager. Replaced by the Flips. I job hunted with good references and great resume. I only got 2 interviews in 6 months, finally landed a shitty call center job. Had to take it because I needed work.

No one is hiring WFH unless you're something special or have certs others cant easily get. Why pay you 20+/hr when they can hire a flip for 2.50/hr. You can do more work? Okay, I can hire 4 flips and its still less than hiring you. If not flips, indians.

62088548
>>62084678
I'm going back to school for cyber security. What certs do you recommend? I have a security clearance which I hope gets me an entry level job at least

62088565
>>62084678
I was in my job for 8 years then Covid hit and we all just went home permanently. There was a 2 year period where we weren’t sure if we were going to come back to the office or not, but finally they said we were permanently remote.

62088593
>>62084678
I took an internship straight out of college (2018) which turned into a software dev job. Am senior dev now. We went fully remote during covid and have been ever since

62088599
>>62088526
This kind of disloyalty should be punishable by public hanging

62088666
>>62088599
yup I agree. my boss' hands were tied, he is still there, the flips are dogshit and theyve had to hire even more to try and cover all the work the American team was doing. They have a flip manager too. Not a single American on the entire ecomm fraud team. Bossman says its only a matter of time until the entire company is flips. I don't have anything against the flips, some of them were really cool. And it's not like they caused me to get laid off. Its the greedy Indian CEO who caused it. And Id much rather hear a Pilipino accent than an Indian accent if I ever had to call in to speak to customer service.

62090239
>>62088548
Grow a neck beard and get fat because it’s worked for every other cyber security worker

62090283

Just look at all the popular YouTube channels. You either need to make ai slop or Candace Owens, Tucker Carlson, Jimmy Dore, Theo Von, Dave Smith type stuff. Anti-Israel content like greyzone, etc

Most people here are thinking working for the man is the answer but the right answer is destroying what the man has built

>No one is hiring WFH unless you're something special or have certs others cant easily get.
I’ve gotten like 5 or 6 wfh jobs between 2022-2026 just by lying on my resume. Getting offers is definitely harder than it was back then but not impossible. I think if anything I’m overqualified, hence why I’ve had so many interviews but not many offers recently, so I just took off one job and slightly extended the timeline on another one.

>>62084678
Be indispensable at your job. Say you're going to move and can either work remote from home or find another job. Watch them find a way to let you work remote. Simple as.

62090748
Mine came naturally after years of working in the tech field. If you study Azure a.k.a Entra, Exchange Admin, Sharepoint Admin, InTune, ETC, you'll move from the nerd who tells retards how to reboot their computers to endpoint management or some kind of administrator. Most of those jobs can be done from anywhere. You just get a company laptop, connect to a VPN.

62090930
>>62084718
based

62091196
Got an interview coming up for a WFH job as a claims adjuster. Paid training and all. Starting off is 64k a year. Much better than my shitty county job. I hope I get it.

62091298
>>62091196

Welding or something else actually valuable

>And that would be even more retarded
No. Payments often go through intermediaries, fresh wallets, or on-chain protocols at places like Qeshm Island.
Iran has a dedicated crypto exchange window to quickly swap incoming stables into rials, other assets, or offshore accounts before full detection.
They use Tron heavily (fast, cheap, popular for USDT) and move funds fast through layered exchanges or bridges.
Some reporting explicitly notes a preference for stablecoins over BTC for volatility reasons, but with BTC as a backup precisely because it has no freeze button. Recent updates show mixed signals: some vessels pay in yuan, others in stables, and some sources emphasize Bitcoin to avoid the off-switch.
No better realistic alternatives right now. Traditional banking is closed to them for sanctioned entities.
Barter or pure cash is slow and logistically messy for high-volume tanker tolls.
Pure decentralized stables (like DAI) lack the liquidity and easy on-ramps/off-ramps at this scale.
Their overall crypto ecosystem is already deep into stablecoins — Iran's Central Bank has historically accumulated hundreds of millions in USDT for trade and rial support. The IRGC has moved billions through similar channels for proxies and evasion. It's a known tool, even if imperfect.

item.title: Financially Fucked By My Father — 60 replies — 8.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Financially Fucked By My Father

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-04-09 11:18 UTC

OP

My dad has no life insurance. Still owes $75k on the mortgage with 8 years to go. He only has $5k in the bank and $5k in his 401k at 63 years old.

He doesn't do ANY maintenance on the house. The basement hasn't had a dehumidifier in it for 7 years and it's full of rat shit and probably mold. The roof is overdue by 2 years and it'll cost about $20k for a new roof. Water collects around the foundation of the house when it rains and he doesn't do anything about it. The water filters need to be changed twice a month, but he only does it once a month, so there's rust in both bathrooms. The master bathroom bathtub drain leaks into the basement and he hasn't fixed it in 4 years, so he just showers in the other bathroom. The kitchen sink leaks water. The fridge is brown from dirt and grime. The stove is covered in dirt and grime. The microwave above the stove is broken, so he has a separate microwave on the counter. The carpet is 20 years old and destroyed. The laminate flooring is peeling away. The window sills are bubbled and peeled from water damage. The furnace is 20 years old. The garage has no electricity running to it and the garage door is broken. He doesn't even fix that when he used to install and repair garage doors as a job for 30 years. There are cobwebs in the living areas. The porches are falling apart and it's a tripping hazard every time you walk up the stairs.

All he does is get drunk, jackoff to porn, blast music and mow the lawn.

Having a parent that is lazy, financially retarded and unhygienic will screw you over when it comes to owning and maintaining a home. I refuse to put $100k into the house to make it enjoyable to live in, especially when he's still living there.

My mom is awesome though and she lives with me.

Are you in the same situation, or did you get lucky enough to have intelligent, hardworking parents that actually care about your future finances?

I once rented a house with a 34 year old furnace. Not like olden immortal boiler or something, forced air natty has with an electric blower. 34 years. I found out the landlord didn't want to replace it as it would mean converting to a high efficiency unit as the old 80% units aren't legal to install here anymore. The high efficiency unit install was going to run upwards of 10k as there was no simple way to install the new pvc flue. Needless to say I did not stay there long. I found out when there was an ignition issue, which he had fixed, then curiously insisted on installing carbon monoxide detectors in every room.

62083898
at least you have one parent that owns some sort of house, even if its some shitty trailer. You don't have to act like you have this giant burden hanging over you. Just sell the turd and wash your hands of it. You're not complaining about inheriting a free house, are you? I wish I could say the same about my dad. He's in his 60s and owns nothing, not even a shitty trailer.

62084981
>>62083742
Good thing you got out of there. Wtf.

>>62083898
I like the land, the street and the town it's in. It's just the house is a total piece of shit and an eyesore, simply because of poor decision after poor decision. Trying to communicate anything to him is like trying to communicate to a toddler.

62088630
>>62084981

But it's still a manufactured home, whether it's in good shape or not. That being said, there's always something a house needs fixed when it's time to change hands. So your options are to take these repairs on yourself after he croaks and move in, or sell it as is. You must really like that house and location to be willing to consider moving there with all that's wrong with it. Most people that inherit a house with that many problems just sell it. Find something you like better and put the money towards that. And for what it's worth, I would try and not worry about the house. It sounds like your dad is starting to lose his mind slowly, as do most people in their later years. It probably won't get better and there's nothing you can do to fix it. It's better to accept the situation for what it is rather than stress out about how you're going to get through to your elderly dad to stop fucking up his house. It's gonna happen no matter how much you try to intervene. Once your dad passes away and the smoke clears, that's when it's time to worry about this stuff.

62090019
>>62081778
Top 7% for my age likewise and both of my parents managed to leave me with nothing at their ages. Granted they're a bit younger than 63.

>hurr durr im not making any money!
Because the old tard didn't understand that he: one had one less major expense to worry about and two was having someone else pay equity for him. He could easily be renting out that home for nearly x2.5 what the mortgage originally was. Mom never amounted to anything and still asks me for money so she can dump it on the next fitness trend and a new vehicle every other year. Truly unfathomable that they don't any concept of retirement savings and its a bloody miracle I managed to secure anything at all. I don't think I could ever tell them how much money I have because I know for certain the first thing they'd want to do is blow it on some useless shit that they'll forget about in 5 years.

62084650
>>62075553
It creats demand for dollars around the world. That keeps the price of dollars higher than it otherwise would be since new supply of dollars can be sent to other countries in exchange for manufacturered goods. This means the US doesn't need to have a massive manufacturing base at home which means less pollution and in general high stress and health risk jobs. Instead the US economy can function by basically finding various mechanisms to just hand the US population money which they can spend and eventually the money finds its way abroad. This creates a large service economy where most people don't actually do any "real work" like making shit. Now their job is just a way to inject money into the domestic economy so that it can then be sent over seas and then those people can use that money to buy things like US bonds(both corporate and government), oil, US stocks and other things that are traded in dollars. Jobs end up being less stressful, you work fewer hours, and there's lots of room to climb up since career ladders are just social structures to hand out money rather than actually give you real responsibility. There's still plenty of real jobs of course, but an insane amount of US jobs are the kind where you just sit at a computer and read emails and then send another email to someone else and that person tells the floormanager of a factory to tell their immigrant labor that there's overtime this weekend.

So Americans enjoy a high standard of living even compared to other developed countries. People own cars, get cheap fuel, imported goods are cheap, there's options to save with high returns and so on. If the dollar stopped being the reserve currency all the dollars that's currently floating around in the world economy would have little use outside the US and they'd flow back into the domestic economy. The dollar loses most of its value and the standard of living of Americans drops since they're losing access to foreign goods.
